title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
summary: Aluminum prices have gained 13% in 2025 and are trading at three-year highs on the LME, driven by China's prevention of overcapacity in metal production and supply concerns from boxside grades. The market deficit in Europe stands at 1.8 million tons, with demand continuing to grow, suggesting higher prices going forward.

title: Aluminum prices are surging. Here's how companies are handling ...
summary: Aluminum prices on the London Metal Exchange have surged over 13% since the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28, reaching levels not seen since 2022. In 2026 alone, the commodity has risen approximately 19%, exerting financial strain on manufacturers across automotive and beverage sectors.

title: Aluminum Prices Have Surged Nearly 90% Since the Iran War ...
summary: Aluminum prices have surged nearly 90% following the Iran War, with Kaiser Aluminum beating EPS estimates by 90.49%. Metal Exchange prices recently exceeded previous highs, reflecting strong market momentum amid geopolitical tensions.

title: Aluminum Market Trends - S&P Global
summary: Extruders remain cautious for 2026 after an up-and-down 2025, with trade uncertainty and supply tightness pushing the Midwest Premium above $1/lb. The report highlights ongoing market volatility and supply constraints affecting aluminum pricing trends.

title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian ...
summary: Aluminum prices closed near levels not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks on two Middle Eastern producers over the weekend. The geopolitical escalation has triggered significant price increases in the global aluminum market.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. This move reflects a strategic response to current market pressures and policy changes affecting the US aluminum industry.

title: Aluminum Rally Builds as War Risks Threaten Global Supply Chains
summary: Middle East disruptions, constrained global production, and China's capacity limits are reshaping the aluminum market. China has imposed a self-imposed cap of 45.5 million tons, which it is expected to reach this year.

title: Aluminum hits four-year high on Mideast supply loss
summary: Aluminum prices have reached a four-year high due to supply losses in the Middle East, with deficits expected to persist through 2026 and 2027. The market is projected to remain unbalanced until 2028.

title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks
summary: Aluminum prices approached levels unseen since 2022 after Iranian attacks disrupted regional production. The region accounts for roughly 23% of ex-China global production, significantly impacting supply dynamics.

title: Company targets extra 75,000 tonnes of annual primary aluminium production by end of 2026
summary: A company is targeting to bring an extra 75,000 tonnes of annual primary aluminium production onstream by the end of 2026. This involves reactivating potline one at their facility to increase output.

title: Steel rose to 3054 CNY/T on July 3, 2026, up 0.53% from the previous day
summary: Steel prices increased to 3,054 CNY/T on July 3, 2026, marking a 0.53% rise from the prior day. Over the past month, steel prices have declined by 3.08%, though they remain 0.13% higher than the previous month's level[1].

title: American Steel Just Got More Expensive. Buyers Blame Tariffs.
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs sold steel at an average of $1,015 per ton in Q2, up from $980 in Q1, as tariffs on steel and aluminum drive price hikes. These increases are passed to domestic producers in industries like automotive and military equipment, with U.S. steel prices now at $2,000 per metric ton[2].

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: Steel prices have not dropped despite ongoing war, while diesel prices have surged 50% since the Iran war began. The current steel pricing upcycle has lasted nearly nine months, with prices near $1,100 and potential to reach $1,500 for hot-rolled coil[3].

title: US Midwest Domestic Hot-Rolled Coil Steel Futures Historical Data
summary: Over the past 12 months, U.S. Steel Coil futures have changed by 30.62%. Today's trading range is between 1,153.00 and 1,156.00, reflecting continued volatility in the domestic steel market[4].

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
summary: Current prices include Cold Rolled Steel at $1,347/ton, Coated Steel at $1,455/ton, and Steel Plate at $1,286/ton. Nucor's weekly Consumer Spot Price data is also referenced, indicating ongoing market monitoring for potential price drops[6].

title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
summary: Steelmaking capacity is expected to rise for a seventh consecutive year, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025, exacerbating trade friction and depressing prices due to oversupply. The report pegged 2025 surplus capacity at 680 million mt, with China contending with a steel surplus exacerbated by poor domestic demand and minimal efforts to reduce capacity[1].

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: Capacity utilization has slipped to 74.6%, falling short of the administration's stated goal of 80.0%, while the domestic steel industry has seen a slowdown in production. However, recent short-term indicators suggest signs of stabilization with production levels rising 4.1% above the year-to-date average for the week of May 10, 2025[2].

title: United States Steel Production
summary: Steel Production in the United States increased to 7500 Thousand Tonnes in May from 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April of 2026, according to data from the World Steel Association. This marks a rebound from the unchanged production level of 7200 Thousand Tonnes recorded in April[3].

title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand
summary: Twenty-one million tons of additional steel production capacity has been announced, placed under construction, or completed in the past four years, marking the most significant expansion of steelmaking capabilities seen in many years. The United States stands on the brink of a renewed era in steel production, but it now requires clientele to sustain this growth as manufacturers construct new facilities[5].

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: In the week ending on June 27, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,842,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 79.8 percent. This data reflects the most recent operational metrics for the U.S. domestic steel industry[6].

title: JSW Steel Commences Steel Plant Project
summary: JSW Steel announced it has begun development of its up to ₹16,350 crore Rayalaseema Steel project in Andhra Pradesh, India. This major initiative aims to expand steel production capacity with a focus on modern, sustainable manufacturing practices[1].

title: JSW Steel USA Mingo Junction Successfully Rolls First Fully Degassed Slab
summary: JSW Steel USA Mingo Junction announced the successful rolling of the first fully degassed slab on its new twin-tank vacuum degasser and rebuilt caster strand with dynamic soft reduction. This technological advancement improves steel quality and supports energy-efficient production processes[1].

title: Coolbrook Introduces RotoDynamic Heater™ for Industrial Process Heating
summary: Coolbrook introduced its newest solution for industrial process heating: the RotoDynamic Heater™ (RDH), designed to enhance efficiency in steel manufacturing. This innovation supports decarbonization goals by reducing energy consumption in industrial heating applications[1].

title: Metal 3D Printing Extends the Service Life of Steel Components
summary: A new Swiss Government press release highlights how metal 3D printing technology is extending the service life of steel components, contributing to sustainability in the steel industry. This innovation reduces material waste and supports circular economy principles in steel manufacturing[3].

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices edged down 0.20% today, settling at $16,273.31 per ton globally and ¥110,479 per ton in China. The live chart tracks battery-grade and stainless steel nickel pricing trends.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
summary: Nickel futures rose on Tuesday, with the current price at 16,392.38 compared to a previous close of 16,265.63. The market shows upward momentum in nickel futures trading.

title: Nickel rose to 16,360 USD/T on July 3, 2026
summary: Nickel prices rose to 16,360 USD/T on July 3, 2026, up 0.40% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has fallen 12.21%, but it remains 7.21% higher than the same period last year.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia plans to cut nickel output in 2026 to support prices and has reversed its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility. This strategic shift aims to stabilize the global nickel market amid fluctuating demand and production costs.

title: Nickel fell to $16,300 per tonne, its lowest level since late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine production
summary: Nickel prices dropped to $16,300 per tonne, marking the lowest level since late December, as Indonesia signals it will allow a significant rise in nickel mine production. This anticipated increase in output is expected to further influence global supply dynamics and price trends.

title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply
summary: Nickel prices have surged past $1,000 per ton, a dramatic climb from $150 just 18 months ago, driven by Indonesia's tightening supply and rising production expenses. Globally, LME nickel inventories have dropped by 10,000 tons over the past two months, signaling a rapidly tightening market.

title: Italy's Tenaris posts 2% rise in quarterly net sales
summary: Italian steel pipe maker Tenaris reported a 2% increase in third-quarter net sales, driven by higher sales volumes in North America. The growth was attributed to stable drilling activity in the region.

title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast
summary: Luxembourg-based Tenaris has acquired control of Fast Srl, a Bergamo-based company that manufactures equipment for oil and gas extraction. This move strengthens Tenaris's presence in Italy's industrial sector.

title: Prysmian to build Italy-Tunisia cables at Naples plant
summary: Prysmian will produce cables for a submarine power link between Italy and Tunisia at its facility near Naples, the company said Monday. This project reinforces Prysmian's role in Italy's energy infrastructure and expands its submarine cable portfolio.

title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff set-up
summary: Prysmian is poised to gain from recent copper tariff adjustments in the United States, which impose taxes on imported cables, as stated by the company’s CEO. The Italian cable manufacturer expects improved pricing and margins due to its domestic copper procurement strategy.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid U.S. copper tariffs
summary: Shares of Prysmian surged by as much as 4% on Wednesday, driven by anticipation that the leading global cable manufacturer could gain from U.S. tariffs on copper. The company procures copper domestically and is likely to transfer increased costs to its clients.

title: Prysmian Boosts Use of Recycled Copper in U.S. Amid Trade Tension
summary: Spurred by tariff fears, Prysmian has increased the amount of recycled copper it uses in the U.S. to about 60% of the total. This shift highlights how trade disputes between the U.S. and China are reshaping commodity movement and supply chain strategies.
